Title: Re: OneDrive No Longer Working
Notes:
1. Verified credentials:

    • Confirmed login to https://webmail.earthlink.net was successful using cmaggio01@sprynet.com.

    • Confirmed that the same password does not authenticate through Outlook.

  2. Attempted automatic Outlook setup:

    • Outlook detected the address but returned “This username may be incorrect” when trying to connect via Microsoft 365 login flow.

  3. Tested manual IMAP configurations:

    • Tried the following IMAP settings:

      • imap.earthlink.net, Port 993 (SSL/TLS) → Connection dropped

      • imap.earthlink.net, Port 143 (STARTTLS) → Encryption handshake failed

  4. Tested POP configurations (legacy Sprynet settings):

    • pop.earthlink.net and pop.sprynet.com with Ports 110 (no encryption) → Server reached but authentication failed

    • Outgoing server smtpauth.earthlink.net tested on Ports 25 and 587 (no encryption / STARTTLS)

  5. Confirmed mail access via WebMail:

    • Verified full mailbox access in EarthLink WebMail.

    • Confirmed account storage status (81% used) and active forwarding settings.

  6. Reviewed WebMail Preferences and Account Portal:

    • Checked Preferences → Account Security and EarthLink Account Portal for POP/IMAP or “third-party access” settings — no explicit toggle found for enabling email client connections.

  7. Outcome:

    • OneDrive successfully authenticates using Microsoft credentials.

    • Outlook fails to connect to Sprynet mail servers even with verified credentials and correct configurations.

Current Status:

  • OneDrive: Working correctly with cmaggio01@sprynet.com.

  • Outlook: Unable to authenticate or connect to incoming POP/IMAP servers.

  • EarthLink WebMail: Functional via browser.

  • Next Action Needed: EarthLink to verify whether POP/IMAP access is enabled for Sprynet domain accounts or if special credentials (app password) are required for external clients.

Title: OneDrive No Longer Working
Notes:
As per Handover – Earthlink Email Configuration (User: Carol Maggio)

User is using an Earthlink email address cmaggio01@sprynet.com and wanted to configure it in the Outlook app or alternatively set up forwarding to Carol@siegeconsulting.com
.

Initially, forwarding was not working.

User mentioned she was busy and requested a later session.

Reconnected later and successfully configured the Earthlink account using IMAP settings.

However, when sending test emails, messages were not received in the Outlook app.

Upon checking the Earthlink webmail client, it was found that all new incoming emails were being delivered to the Spam folder. Added the sender addresses to the address book, after which emails started arriving correctly.

Sent multiple test emails — confirmed working fine.

Next, noticed that emails sent from Carol@siegeconsulting.com to cmaggio01@sprynet.com were failing.
Logged into the Microsoft 365 Admin Center and found that the sprynet.com tenant was blocked. Removed it from the block list, and after resending test emails, messages were successfully delivered.

Mail forwarding was also set up and verified to be working properly.

Currently, waiting to confirm with the user, but she is not available at this time.

Title: Email not working
Notes: Connected to the user’s machine after receiving an email from Carol regarding an email issue. Upon investigation, found that high system utilization was preventing Outlook from syncing. Cleared temporary and prefetch files, then rebooted the system. After the restart, asked the user to verify functionality. The user confirmed that Outlook is now working fine and all emails are syncing properly. Hence, closing this ticket.

Title: System slowness issue
Notes: Received a support call from Carol with SGC regarding a system issue after powering on the computer. Connected to the user’s machine (CAROL) and found that applications were not responding. Disabled unnecessary startup applications and increased the page file size to improve virtual memory. After these changes, the system is now working fine.

Title: RE: OneDrive No Longer Working
Notes: Recieved email on support from Carol for having system boot delay issue and application not responding issue. Upon investigation, Event Viewer logs revealed multiple DistributedCOM (Event ID 10010) errors caused by Windows components (Microsoft.WindowsCommunicationsApps) failing to register with DCOM within the required timeout, contributing to extremely slow startup performance and delayed application response. Logged into the system via ScreenConnect and navigated to HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Ole, where the EnableDCOM registry value was set to “Y”. Updated this value to “N” to disable unnecessary DCOM activation retries. Additionally, ran sfc /scannow and dism /online /cleanup-image /restorehealth to repair any corrupted or missing system files. Currently SFC scan is running.

Title: RE: OneDrive No Longer Working
Notes:
Handover received from the previous shift. Upon investigation, the Event Viewer logs showed multiple DistributedCOM (Event ID 10010) errors caused by Windows components (Microsoft.WindowsCommunicationsApps) failing to register with DCOM within the required timeout. This contributed to extremely slow startup performance and delayed application responses.

Connected to the system via ScreenConnect and navigated to:
HKEY_LOCAL_MACHINE\SOFTWARE\Microsoft\Ole
The EnableDCOM registry value was set to "Y". Updated the value to "N" to disable unnecessary DCOM activation retries.

Subsequently, ran the following system repair commands to fix any corrupted or missing files:

sfc /scannow  
dism /online /cleanup-image /restorehealth

Rebooted the computer afterward and asked the user to verify functionality — the system appeared to be working fine.

Later, received another email from the user regarding OneDrive synchronization issues. Reconnected to the system, ran DISM utilities again, and rebooted. The Office 365 suite was also repaired to ensure stable application performance. Again got a mail regarding the excel issue so tried to contact with her on remote chat as well as on mail so waiting for her response.
